annotations.label: Elliptic function

anchor.snippet: A meromorphic function is called an elliptic function

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes the period-lattice infrastructure (`PeriodPair`) and shows the Weierstrass ℘ is meromorphic and periodic, but lacks a general definition of an arbitrary elliptic function.

annotations.label: Period lattice
annotations.note: `PeriodPair.lattice` is the ℤ-span of two ℝ-linearly independent complex periods, matching the article's period lattice.

annotations.label: Fundamental domain
annotations.note: `ZSpan.fundamentalDomain` realizes the standard half-open parallelepiped fundamental domain of a ℤ-lattice basis.

annotations.label: Liouville's 1st theorem

anchor.snippet: A holomorphic elliptic function is constant.

annotations.note: No statement that a holomorphic doubly-periodic function is constant appears in Mathlib (only the classical Liouville theorem for bounded entire functions is imported).

annotations.label: Liouville's 2nd theorem

anchor.snippet: Every elliptic function has finitely many poles

annotations.note: Mathlib has no general statement about residue sums of elliptic functions over a fundamental domain.

annotations.label: Liouville's 3rd theorem

anchor.snippet: A non-constant elliptic function takes on every value the same number of times

annotations.note: No formalization of the order/value-distribution theorem for elliptic functions was found.

annotations.label: Weierstrass ℘-function
annotations.note: `PeriodPair.weierstrassP` defines ℘ via the standard series `∑ (1/(z-l)² - 1/l²)` over the lattice.

annotations.label: ℘ is an even elliptic function
annotations.note: `weierstrassP_neg` gives `℘(-z) = ℘(z)` and `periodic_weierstrassP` plus `meromorphic_weierstrassP` supply the elliptic-function content.

annotations.label: Rational expression in ℘ and ℘'

anchor.snippet: Every elliptic function with respect to a given period lattice

annotations.note: No theorem expressing every elliptic function as a rational function in ℘ and ℘' was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Differential equation for ℘
annotations.note: `derivWeierstrassP_sq` proves `℘'(z)² = 4·℘(z)³ - g₂·℘(z) - g₃` for `z` off the lattice.

annotations.label: Field of elliptic functions isomorphism

anchor.snippet: the field of elliptic functions is isomorphic to the field

annotations.note: Mathlib does not construct the field of elliptic functions or its isomorphism with `ℂ(X)[Y]/(Y² - 4X³ + g₂X + g₃)`.

annotations.label: Abel elliptic functions

anchor.snippet: Abel discovered elliptic functions by taking the inverse function

annotations.note: Abel's inverse-elliptic-integral functions are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Jacobi elliptic functions
annotations.note: Mathlib has Jacobi theta functions but no Jacobi elliptic functions (sn, cn, dn).
